articles.title: YOUR BRAND HERE
articles.summary: If your audience is in tech, they read TLDR. Capture their attention with a native ad placed at the top, middle or bottom of TLDR newsletters. Learn more [4] about running your first campaign with us.

articles.title: 26 PREDICTIONS FOR SOCIAL MEDIA MARKETING IN 2025
articles.summary: Meta will focus on expanding AI-driven content in 2025, pushing users to generate AI images and engage with bots. Expect more AR features across Facebook and Instagram, including AR glasses integration and immersive experiences. Meta will also enhance video content, mimicking TikTok's short-form style, and prioritize AI improvements for advertising. Meanwhile, Threads may loosen political restrictions to foster real-time, topical discussions, and Snapchat will face challenges in competing with Meta and Apple's AR advancements.

articles.title: THE STATE OF MARKETING OPERATIONS PROFESSIONALS IS OK, BUT IT'S GOING TO GET MUCH, MUCH BETTER
articles.summary: Marketing operations has evolved into a key role, with professionals earning six-figure salaries and a growing pool of experienced talent. The core responsibilities focus on data, technology, and process management, with a strong emphasis on data quality, enrichment, and integration. Integration is the top factor when evaluating new tools, with 81.3% of professionals prioritizing it. Despite advances, many companies still have low digital maturity, creating significant opportunities for growth.

articles.title: HOW TO WRITE A HOOK: 7+ BOLD WAYS TO INSTANTLY GRAB ATTENTION
articles.summary: Techniques for grabbing attention include promise hooks that offer benefits, proof hooks that establish trust, and empathy hooks that connect emotionally. Statistic hooks use surprising data, while massive mistake hooks build trust by sharing relatable failures. Story hooks engage with compelling narratives, and question hooks spark curiosity to keep readers interested. Each approach aims to create intrigue and sustain reader engagement.

articles.title: 7 BLACK FRIDAY MARKETING IDEAS
articles.summary: Ideas for Black Friday include personalizing email campaigns based on customer behavior, running gratitude-focused social media contests, and collaborating with local businesses for joint promotions to strengthen community ties. Offering thank-you giveaways to loyal customers provides a meaningful alternative to discounts, while sharing authentic gratitude stories on social media adds warmth. Fun holiday experiences like scavenger hunts or trivia make shopping more enjoyable.

articles.title: INSIDE STATSIG'S PRICING PHILOSOPHY
articles.summary: Statsig's transparent pricing strategy, charging only for features that incur costs or add customer value, has fueled its growth since its 2021 founding. The company reached $25M ARR through product experimentation and positioning itself as a comprehensive growth platform. Statsig offers three main plans: Developer (free), Pro, and Enterprise. Its 3-Part Tariff structure, based on event volume, ensures affordability and scalability. By offering core features for free and scaling with customer needs, Statsig encourages seamless, cost-effective upgrades.

articles.title: HOW MARKETERS ARE USING CUSTOM AI AGENTS TO 10X THEIR PRODUCTIVITY
articles.summary: You.com's whitepaper [10] provides an industry-wide perspective on how custom AI agents are drastically boosting marketing output and quality, while streamlining workflows. You'll learn: simple steps to deploy custom AI agents across your workflows, dozens of use-case scenarios, and industry breakdowns of how/where AI agents are

articles.title: CHATGPT PROMPT FOR YOUR ICP
articles.summary: A templated prompt that helps you understand why your ideal customer would want to buy what you sell.

articles.title: INSTAGRAM SAYS THAT IT DOES NOT REDUCE THE REACH OF SPONSORED CONTENT
articles.summary: Instagram doesn't deliberately downrank posts tagged as sponsored content in order to force brands to pay for reach.

articles.title: HOW MUCH ARE YOU OVERSPENDING ON PAID ADVERTISING?
articles.summary: Improving conversion rates (CRO) can reduce PPC ad costs or increase profits. For example, doubling a site's conversion rate from 2% to 4% can boost profit contributions by 4.5X, enabling cost savings of nearly 78% or reinvestment for growth. CRO enhancements benefit all traffic sources and compound to double conversions while also refining PPC strategies to optimize costs.

articles.title: YOU DON'T NEED TO BUILD A PERSONAL BRAND
articles.summary: The priority early in a career should be on producing great work rather than building a personal brand. A strong reputation develops naturally from genuine contributions and results as personal branding is a "lagging indicator" of success. Sharing valuable insights publicly can enhance reputation but it should stem from real experience, not attention-seeking. Delivering impactful, value-driven work ultimately creates an authentic and lasting brand.

articles.title: WHY GREAT MARKETING IS RISKY AS HELL
articles.summary: Modern marketing often follows algorithms, leading brands to replicate successful patterns instead of taking bold risks. Ahrefs broke this mold by launching a children's book on SEO based on a simple idea: connecting with industry parents in an unexpected way. This gut-driven move provided a unique gift and refreshed the brand's image.

articles.title: WHY GENERATIONAL STEREOTYPES ARE FAILING MARKETERS AND HOW TO MOVE PAST LABELS
articles.summary: Generational labels like "Gen Z" or "Millennials" often oversimplify and stereotype groups, overlooking the complex mix of factors that shape behaviors and values. Critics argue that such research is often more “clickbait” than evidence-based. These labels ignore life stage, era, and cohort differences, leading to one-dimensional marketing. Marketers should prioritize deeper research to avoid assumptions and achieve more effective, ethical results.

articles.title: DYNAMIC MENU, SAVVY MARKETING KEY TO TACO BELL SUCCESS, EXPERTS SAY
articles.summary: 60% of Taco Bell sales are from its app, where users are targeted with limited-time offers of familiar and experimental items.
